metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'Four-Criterion test', 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception', and 'VALID-patterns-not-to-flag' vocabulary inline in `code-review-tests.md` (or in a linked spec that is co-located), so an executor has a complete decision rule without external lookup.
findings.finding: The plan's 'tests' overlay references 'Four-Criterion test' and 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception' as named decision rules that the overlay must embed, but neither term is defined anywhere in the codebase or the plan — an executor implementing this overlay has no vocabulary to work from.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add verify_commands (or equivalent AC gate) to each child specifying how to run and confirm the recall + no-fire eval passes before the child is closed.
findings.finding: Children 1ead-6d53-8f07-4c24 (llm-prompts), 3cb3-275f-fbc1-49e3 (api-compat), a010-aec8-c088-4314 (performance), d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119 (security), and f894-ebd4-e841-404f (db-migrations) each carry no verify_commands, while parent AC2 requires a passing eval for each overlay. There is no mechanism specified in any child to actually run or gate the recall + no-fire eval, making 'passing eval' unverifiable at merge time.
findings.location: child 1ead-6d53-8f07-4c24, child 3cb3-275f-fbc1-49e3, child a010-aec8-c088-4314, child d71f-24ab-dc4c-4119, child f894-ebd4-e841-404f, child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define (in child 0f8f or a shared artifact) the eval spec schema — fixture format, pass/fail criteria expression, and naming convention — so all five downstream children can conform to a verified pattern.
findings.finding: No child defines the eval spec schema, fixture format, or pass/fail criteria that the tests child is supposed to 'prove as the pattern' for the five downstream children to replicate. The five dependent children implicitly consume an undefined artifact from the tests child, creating risk that each child independently invents an inconsistent eval structure.
findings.location: child 0f8f-ec8a-8c93-4fc5 (and implicitly all five dependent children: 1ead, 3cb3, a010, d71f, f894)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to child 0f8f's AC or deliverables: 'Produces a documented eval spec schema (fixture format + pass/fail structure) as the canonical pattern for the five downstream overlays to follow.' Reference this artifact explicitly in each of the five dependent children's ACs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the recall + no-fire eval mechanism for each overlay: specify what structure the eval takes (e.g., a golden-diff fixture + expected-findings list), how it is executed (manual run, CI step, offline test), where the artifact lives, and what threshold constitutes 'passing' — so the AC is verifiable at closure.
findings.finding: The plan's AC commits to 'a passing recall + no-fire eval' per overlay, but neither the plan nor the codebase defines the eval mechanism — what structure the eval takes, how it is executed, what artifact it produces, and what 'passing' means. Without a specified mechanism the AC is unverifiable, and an implementer has no actionable guidance on how to construct or run the eval.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether code-review-tests.md's antipattern taxonomy is derived from or intentionally independent of plan_review_E5.md, and document that relationship (or consolidate) to prevent definitional drift.
findings.finding: The tests overlay enrichment (change-detector, tautological, over/under-mocking, source-grepping, existence-only anti-patterns) directly overlaps with an existing, well-developed taxonomy in plan_review_E5.md. The plan does not acknowledge this prior art, risking inconsistency between the plan-review and code-review test antipattern rules.
findings.suggested_fix: Reference plan_review_E5.md as the canonical prior art for the test-antipattern definitions in the tests overlay, and either import its definitions verbatim or explicitly note the intentional differences.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Create `.rebar/evals/rebar-code-review-<overlay>.eval.yaml` (or equivalent packaged eval specs) for each of the six overlay prompt ids, containing at minimum a recall case and a no-fire case, so AC #2 is verifiable at landing.
findings.finding: The plan's AC #2 requires each overlay to 'land with a passing recall + no-fire eval', but no eval spec files exist for any of the six new overlay prompt ids (`rebar-code-review-tests`, `rebar-code-review-security`, etc.) — the eval infrastructure referenced by AC #2 has no backing artifacts.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite AC 2 to specify a measurable eval definition: e.g., a minimum number of recall test cases per overlay, the command or CI step that runs them, and a concrete threshold for 'no over-suppression' (e.g., zero suppression of findings that match the overlay's own recall patterns).
findings.finding: AC 2 ('Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') does not define what 'passing' means: no threshold, no test-case count, no eval mechanism (script, CI job, manual review), and 'do not over-suppress' has no measurable definition. An agent cannot determine whether this criterion is met without subjective judgment.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC 2: 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.'
findings.suggested_fix: Example rewrite: 'Each overlay includes ≥ 3 recall cases and ≥ 2 no-fire cases; `rebar eval --overlay <name>` reports 0 missed-recall and 0 false-suppression failures in CI.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define what 'passing' means for the recall + no-fire eval (e.g., reference the existing eval harness/format, specify minimum recall hit-rate and maximum false-suppression rate, or name the script/command that executes the eval).
findings.finding: The acceptance criterion 'each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' lacks an operationalized definition of 'passing' — no threshold, format spec, or reference to an existing eval harness is provided, leaving the end-state-observable proof under-specified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to Scope or AC such as: 'Eval format follows `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/eval-format.md` (or equivalent); passing = all recall fixtures trigger AND no VALID-pattern fixture is flagged. Run via `make eval-overlays`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify 'dso' explicitly — provide a file path, document title, or inline enumeration of the referenced concerns so an agent can proceed without clarification.
findings.finding: The plan repeatedly references 'dso' (e.g., 'dso content', 'dso's 8 red-team concerns', '8 AI-advantaged concerns') as the authoritative source for overlay content, but never identifies what 'dso' is, where it lives, or how to access it. An executing agent cannot write the security or performance overlays without this source material.
findings.location: ## What — security bullet ('dso's 8 red-team concerns') and performance bullet ('8 AI-advantaged concerns')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Source material' sub-section or inline list: e.g., 'dso = src/rebar/docs/domain-specific-overlays.md § Security (8 concerns listed there)' or enumerate the 8 concerns directly in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Align terminology for the FP half of overlays across all sections — pick one term (e.g. 'FP-guards') and use it consistently in Why, What, AC1, and AC2 so 'no-fire eval' is explicitly described as the test that validates the FP-guards.
findings.finding: Three different terms — 'FP-discrimination' (Why), 'FP-guards' (What/AC1), and 'no-fire' (What body / AC2) — are used for the same concept across sections, creating a terminology mismatch that could confuse an implementer cross-checking AC1 against AC2.
findings.location: Why section ('FP-discrimination'); What section ('FP-guards', 'no-fire eval'); AC1 ('FP-guards'); AC2 ('no-fire eval')
findings.suggested_fix: In AC2, replace 'passing recall + no-fire eval' with 'passing recall eval and no-fire (FP-guards) eval' to make the correspondence to AC1's 'FP-guards' explicit; update Why to use 'FP-guards' instead of 'FP-discrimination'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for enriched overlays (e.g., per-overlay feature flag, staged rollout one overlay at a time with a defined revert commit strategy, or an expand-contract sequence that keeps old overlay content until the new eval passes in production).
findings.finding: The plan enriches six existing overlays (changing their behavioral effect on the Pass-2 review pipeline) but states no explicit rollback or back-out path if enriched overlays regress pipeline behavior (e.g., new FP-guards suppress previously-correct findings, or recall patterns generate noise that degrades review quality).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Each overlay lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; overlays do not over-suppress.'
findings.suggested_fix: Adopt a one-overlay-at-a-time rollout with a stated 'revert to prior commit' trigger condition (e.g., recall score drops or suppression rate exceeds X% on the no-fire eval corpus). Document this as an AC: 'If post-merge eval regresses, the overlay is reverted to its pre-enrichment state within one working day.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at least one concrete validation mechanism to the ACs — e.g. a dogfooding run replaying a set of historic PRs and comparing recall/FP counts before and after, or an explicit reviewer sign-off that the new patterns surface issues the old overlays missed.
findings.finding: No concrete validation mechanism links the overlay enrichment to an observable improvement for the end-user (code-reviewer / rebar user): the ACs describe only internal system evals ('passing recall + no-fire eval') with no before/after workflow comparison, operational metric target, or dogfooding step.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC such as: 'Dogfooding: replay N representative past PRs through each enriched overlay and confirm recall improves (≥1 previously-missed issue surfaced) and FP rate does not increase relative to the un-enriched baseline.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and link the 'dso' source (internal doc, named standard, or prior art) for each overlay's domain content, and verify that the named constructs ('Four-Criterion test', 'Rule-5 structural-artifact exception', '8 red-team concerns', '8 AI-advantaged concerns') are either cited from an existing document or explicitly authored as new canonical definitions in this work.
findings.finding: The plan repeatedly cites 'dso' as the authoritative source for domain content across multiple overlays (security: 'dso's 8 red-team concerns'; performance: '8 AI-advantaged concerns'; tests: 'full behavioral-testing standard') but never identifies what 'dso' is — an internal doc, a named standard (OWASP, CWE), or a prior system. This is an unverified capability assertion: if 'dso' does not exist or is incomplete, the overlay enrichment delivers wrong/incomplete coverage.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the DET exclusion criteria inline in `code-review-security.md` (e.g., 'do not flag patterns already detected by gitleaks secret-scan or opengrep rule X') so the boundary is self-contained and executable without external lookup.
findings.finding: The plan's 'security' overlay references 'gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria by name' as the authority for hard exclusions, but the DET schema/enum value vocabulary is nowhere defined in the plan or any reachable overlay file — a security overlay executor cannot know what constitutes a DET-covered finding to exclude.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'ui-artifact', 'domain-mismatch', and 'contract-drift' with concrete decision rules inline in `code-review-llm-prompts.md`, scoped to rebar's own prompt seam (e.g., what fields/patterns constitute each category for `langfuse_prompt`-keyed overlays).
findings.finding: The plan's 'llm-prompts' overlay names three detection targets ('ui-artifact', 'domain-mismatch', 'contract-drift') as the overlay's concern, but none of these terms have defined vocabularies or decision rules anywhere in the overlay file or referenced specs — an LLM executor has no operationalizable definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit inconclusive-evidence fallback to `code-review-llm-prompts.md`: specify whether the overlay should emit an `insufficient` note, stay silent, or use a conservative default when contract-drift cannot be confirmed from the diff alone.
findings.finding: The `code-review-llm-prompts.md` overlay specifies no fallback for an inconclusive or failed sub-step result: if the overlay cannot determine whether a prompt change constitutes 'contract-drift' (e.g., due to missing surrounding context), there is no decision rule for what to emit (a finding, an insufficient-evidence note, or silence).
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section: 'If the surrounding schema definition is not in the diff and cannot be confirmed via read_file, emit an evidence record with `evidence: ["ABSENCE: schema definition not readable from diff context"]` rather than guessing the drift direction.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Each of the five non-tests children should explicitly state whether base NOT-flag rules apply to their overlay's findings (mirroring or explicitly contrasting the tests overlay carve-out).
findings.finding: The tests child (0f8f) specifies a unique behavioral rule — 'Base NOT-flag rules do not apply to test-correctness findings' — that has no counterpart in any of the five other overlay children, yet the parent plan only states this exception for the tests overlay. While per-overlay specificity is appropriate, it creates an inconsistency: the other five children do not state whether base NOT-flag rules DO or DO NOT apply to their overlay's findings, leaving the handling of base NOT-flag rules undefined for five of the six overlays.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line clarification to each non-tests child: e.g., 'Base NOT-flag rules apply normally to [dimension] findings (no carve-out)' or document the specific carve-out if one exists.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
